# PumpX2Kit — LLM guide

> A Swift port of the Tandem t:slim X2 / Mobi Bluetooth protocol (from jwoglom's pumpX2), used by the
> faBolus app: it builds pump messages, runs the JPAKE pairing/auth, and speaks BLE — verified
> **byte-exact** against the vendored Java oracle in `vendor/pumpx2-oracle/`. Safety-critical: a wrong
> byte can misdose insulin.

This file is intentionally thin (a map, not a manual) to avoid drifting out of sync. For the real
working guidance, use:

- **`AGENTS.md`** — canonical working guide: SPM layout (`PumpX2Messages` / `PumpX2Auth` / `PumpX2BLE`),
  the oracle-parity golden rule, how to add or change a message, and running the tests
  (`./scripts/test.sh`, needs **JDK 21**).
- **`README.md`** — the modules, using it as a SwiftPM dependency, and the cliparser oracle workflow.
- **`PINNED.md`** — the pinned upstream commit + the on-hardware validation log.
- Tests live in `Tests/PumpX2MessagesTests/` (`OracleParityTests`, `ResponseParityTests`,
  `InitiateBolusExtendedTests`, …).

The one rule that matters most: **never change message bytes without a matching oracle-parity test**,
and `ResponseParser` dispatches on **(characteristic, opcode)** — opcodes are not globally unique.

Two more facts you need before touching safety code:
- **Write policy is risk-tiered, not a boolean.** `WritePolicy` has `.readOnly`, `.allowBenignControl`
  (dismiss-alert / find-my-pump — no therapy change), `.allowNonDelivery`, and `.allowDelivery`; `send()`
  enforces `message.operationRisk ≤ policy.maxRisk` (`OperationRisk`: read < benign < settings <
  destructive < delivery). Don't reach for `.allowDelivery` when a benign tier suffices.
- **Oracle-parity tests need the Java oracle (JDK 21).** `scripts/test.sh` runs the vendored oracle; note
  the current default behavior if the oracle/JDK is absent (see `AGENTS.md`) — a fail-closed CI mode
  (assert the expected oracle case count ran) is tracked as PX-09.
